primary_written_source.citation: Cropley, Steve. "Bugatti Veyron 2005 First Drive." Autocar, 2005.
primary_written_source.notes: 1,001 hp, 253 mph. Michelin and Brembo engineered tires and brakes specifically for 250+ mph sustained speeds.

artifact_types.period-artifact: Contemporaneous artifact from the primary source era — actual magazine page scans, factory press photographs, period advertising, celebrity-delivery news photos, auction-catalog documentation. Cite per its own provenance.
artifact_types.specimen-photograph: Modern photograph of the original vehicle, typically from a motor show, museum, or collection display. Supplements period artifacts by showing the physical car today.

image_primary_sources.creator: Hugh Lunnon
image_primary_sources.description: The Bugatti Veyron at the 2007 Goodwood Festival of Speed — its first major British public appearance after the production launch Cropley covered.
image_primary_sources.chicago_caption: Hugh Lunnon, *Bugatti Veyron at Goodwood Festival of Speed 2007*, 22 June 2007, Wikimedia Commons, CC BY-SA 2.0, https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Goodwood_Festival_of_Speed_2007_(780174993).jpg.
image_primary_sources.chicago_bibliography: Lunnon, Hugh. *Bugatti Veyron at Goodwood Festival of Speed 2007*. Photograph. 22 June 2007. Wikimedia Commons. CC BY-SA 2.0. https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Goodwood_Festival_of_Speed_2007_(780174993).jpg.
image_primary_sources.chicago_footnote_short: Lunnon, *Bugatti Veyron at Goodwood Festival of Speed 2007*.

image_primary_sources.creator: johnantoni
image_primary_sources.description: A privately-owned Veyron on the 2007 Gumball 3000 rally — a period document of the Veyron's arrival in the celebrity-driver circuit.
image_primary_sources.chicago_caption: johnantoni, *Bugatti Veyron at Gumball 3000 2007*, 29 April 2007, Wikimedia Commons, CC BY-SA 2.0, https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Gumball_3000_-_2007_(483283730).jpg.
image_primary_sources.chicago_bibliography: johnantoni. *Bugatti Veyron at Gumball 3000 2007*. Photograph. 29 April 2007. Wikimedia Commons. CC BY-SA 2.0. https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Gumball_3000_-_2007_(483283730).jpg.

image_primary_sources.creator: Axion23
image_primary_sources.description: A Bugatti Veyron driven at speed in 2008 — roughly the period when the car's 253 mph top speed was still the world production-car record and the magazine coverage peaked.
image_primary_sources.chicago_caption: Axion23, *Bugatti Veyron Driving*, 1 November 2008, Wikimedia Commons, CC BY 2.0, https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Bugatti_veyron_driving_(2991971967).jpg.
image_primary_sources.chicago_bibliography: Axion23. *Bugatti Veyron Driving*. Photograph. 1 November 2008. Wikimedia Commons. CC BY 2.0. https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Bugatti_veyron_driving_(2991971967).jpg.

image_primary_sources.creator: Bugatti AG press archive via Supercars.net
image_primary_sources.license: Fair use, educational — manufacturer press photograph
image_primary_sources.description: Veyron chassis 5.0, the validation prototype personally signed off on by Ferdinand Piech in 2005 before series production began at Molsheim. The car Bugatti credits with proving the 1,001 hp and 400 km/h targets were achievable.
image_primary_sources.chicago_caption: Bugatti AG, *Bugatti Veyron Chassis 5.0 Validation Prototype*, 2005, press archive via Supercars.net, https://www.supercars.net/blog/bugatti-veyron-chassis-5-0-the-dream-that-defined-the-hypercar-era/.
image_primary_sources.chicago_bibliography: Bugatti AG. *Bugatti Veyron Chassis 5.0 Validation Prototype*. Press photograph. 2005. Via Supercars.net. https://www.supercars.net/blog/bugatti-veyron-chassis-5-0-the-dream-that-defined-the-hypercar-era/.

image_primary_sources.creator: Bugatti AG press archive via Supercars.net
image_primary_sources.license: Fair use, educational — manufacturer press photograph
image_primary_sources.description: Second angle of chassis 5.0 — the car that ran the 407 km/h validation at Ehra-Lessien with Uwe Novacki at the wheel on 19 April 2005.
image_primary_sources.chicago_caption: Bugatti AG, *Bugatti Veyron Chassis 5.0, Ehra-Lessien Era*, 2005, press archive via Supercars.net, https://www.supercars.net/blog/bugatti-veyron-chassis-5-0-the-dream-that-defined-the-hypercar-era/.
image_primary_sources.chicago_bibliography: Bugatti AG. *Bugatti Veyron Chassis 5.0, Ehra-Lessien Era*. Press photograph. 2005. Via Supercars.net. https://www.supercars.net/blog/bugatti-veyron-chassis-5-0-the-dream-that-defined-the-hypercar-era/.
image_primary_sources.chicago_footnote_short: Bugatti AG, *Bugatti Veyron Chassis 5.0, Ehra-Lessien Era*.

image_primary_sources.creator: Bugatti AG press archive via Supercars.net
image_primary_sources.license: Fair use, educational — manufacturer press photograph
image_primary_sources.description: Engineering detail of the validation prototype showing the quad-turbo W16 installation during development testing.
image_primary_sources.chicago_caption: Bugatti AG, *Bugatti Veyron Chassis 5.0, Engineering Detail*, 2005, via Supercars.net, https://www.supercars.net/blog/bugatti-veyron-chassis-5-0-the-dream-that-defined-the-hypercar-era/.
image_primary_sources.chicago_bibliography: Bugatti AG. *Bugatti Veyron Chassis 5.0, Engineering Detail*. Press photograph. 2005. Via Supercars.net. https://www.supercars.net/blog/bugatti-veyron-chassis-5-0-the-dream-that-defined-the-hypercar-era/.
image_primary_sources.chicago_footnote_short: Bugatti AG, *Bugatti Veyron Chassis 5.0, Engineering Detail*.

image_primary_sources.creator: Bugatti AG press archive via Supercars.net
image_primary_sources.license: Fair use, educational — manufacturer press photograph
image_primary_sources.description: Profile of chassis 5.0, the car whose success enabled the Molsheim Atelier to open on 3 September 2005.
image_primary_sources.chicago_caption: Bugatti AG, *Bugatti Veyron Chassis 5.0, Profile*, 2005, via Supercars.net, https://www.supercars.net/blog/bugatti-veyron-chassis-5-0-the-dream-that-defined-the-hypercar-era/.
image_primary_sources.chicago_bibliography: Bugatti AG. *Bugatti Veyron Chassis 5.0, Profile*. Press photograph. 2005. Via Supercars.net. https://www.supercars.net/blog/bugatti-veyron-chassis-5-0-the-dream-that-defined-the-hypercar-era/.

image_primary_sources.creator: Scuderi Ferrari
image_primary_sources.description: The Bugatti EB 18.3 Chiron concept, unveiled at the 1999 Frankfurt Motor Show — the second in the series of VW-era Bugatti design studies that led directly to the Veyron.
image_primary_sources.chicago_caption: Scuderi Ferrari, *Bugatti EB 18.3 Chiron Concept*, 2007, Wikimedia Commons, Public Domain, https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Bugatti_Chiron_(8162).jpg.
image_primary_sources.chicago_bibliography: Scuderi Ferrari. *Bugatti EB 18.3 Chiron Concept*. Photograph. 2007. Wikimedia Commons. Public Domain. https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Bugatti_Chiron_(8162).jpg.

image_primary_sources.creator: Scuderi Ferrari
image_primary_sources.description: Rear three-quarter of the EB 18.3 Chiron concept, showing the shape that would carry through — with the W16 substitution — to the production Veyron of 2005.
image_primary_sources.chicago_caption: Scuderi Ferrari, *Bugatti EB 18.3 Chiron Concept, Rear*, 2007, Wikimedia Commons, Public Domain, https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Bugatti_Chiron_(8169).jpg.
image_primary_sources.chicago_bibliography: Scuderi Ferrari. *Bugatti EB 18.3 Chiron Concept, Rear*. Photograph. 2007. Wikimedia Commons. Public Domain. https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Bugatti_Chiron_(8169).jpg.
image_primary_sources.chicago_footnote_short: Scuderi Ferrari, *Bugatti EB 18.3 Chiron Concept, Rear*.

image_primary_sources.creator: More Cars
image_primary_sources.description: The Bugatti EB 18.4 Veyron concept — the 1999 Tokyo Motor Show design study, by Hartmut Warkuss and Jozef Kabaň, that first carried the Veyron name.
image_primary_sources.chicago_caption: More Cars, *Bugatti Veyron 18.4 Concept*, 2025, Wikimedia Commons, CC BY 4.0, https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Bugatti_Veyron_18_4_Concept_(54737722934).jpg.
image_primary_sources.chicago_bibliography: More Cars. *Bugatti Veyron 18.4 Concept*. Photograph. 2025. Wikimedia Commons. CC BY 4.0. https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Bugatti_Veyron_18_4_Concept_(54737722934).jpg.

image_primary_sources.creator: GerardM
image_primary_sources.description: A pre-production Bugatti Veyron 16.4 photographed in 2004, one year before the production launch Cropley attended for his 2005 Autocar first-drive.
image_primary_sources.chicago_caption: GerardM, *Bugatti Veyron 16.4 Pre-Production*, 2004, Wikimedia Commons, CC BY-SA 3.0, https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Bugatti_Veyron_16.4_2.JPG.
image_primary_sources.chicago_bibliography: GerardM. *Bugatti Veyron 16.4 Pre-Production*. Photograph. 2004. Wikimedia Commons. CC BY-SA 3.0. https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Bugatti_Veyron_16.4_2.JPG.

image_primary_sources.creator: EricS
image_primary_sources.description: The Bugatti Veyron's second key — the mandatory 'top-speed key' that unlocks the 400 km/h mode by reconfiguring the active aero. A primary artifact of the car's engineering protocol.
image_primary_sources.chicago_caption: EricS, *Bugatti Veyron Top-Speed Key*, 19 October 2005, Wikimedia Commons, Public Domain, https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Schl%C3%BCssel.jpg.
image_primary_sources.chicago_bibliography: EricS. *Bugatti Veyron Top-Speed Key*. Photograph. 19 October 2005. Wikimedia Commons. Public Domain. https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Schl%C3%BCssel.jpg.

image_primary_sources.creator: Michael KR
image_primary_sources.description: Interior of a 2007 Veyron showing the brushed-aluminum cabin architecture — the interior specification Cropley described as resembling a Swiss watch movement.
image_primary_sources.chicago_caption: Michael KR, *Bugatti Veyron Interior*, 23 February 2007, Wikimedia Commons, CC BY-SA 4.0, https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Bugatti_Veyron_Innenraum_489.JPG.
image_primary_sources.chicago_bibliography: Michael KR. *Bugatti Veyron Interior*. Photograph. 23 February 2007. Wikimedia Commons. CC BY-SA 4.0. https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Bugatti_Veyron_Innenraum_489.JPG.

image_primary_sources.wikimedia_title: File:Bugatti Veyron 8.0-litre W16-cylinder engine - Flickr - exfordy.jpg
image_primary_sources.creator: Brian Snelson
image_primary_sources.description: The Veyron's 8.0-litre quad-turbo W16 engine — the powerplant Cropley described as 'an entire W16 lump' delivering 1,001 horsepower through a seven-speed DSG.
image_primary_sources.chicago_caption: Brian Snelson, *Bugatti Veyron 8.0-litre W16 Engine*, 1 June 2008, Wikimedia Commons, CC BY 2.0, https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Bugatti_Veyron_8.0-litre_W16-cylinder_engine_-_Flickr_-_exfordy.jpg.
image_primary_sources.chicago_bibliography: Snelson, Brian. *Bugatti Veyron 8.0-litre W16 Engine*. Photograph. 1 June 2008. Wikimedia Commons. CC BY 2.0. https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Bugatti_Veyron_8.0-litre_W16-cylinder_engine_-_Flickr_-_exfordy.jpg.

image_primary_sources.wikimedia_title: File:W-16 on a Bugatti Veyron - panoramio.jpg
image_primary_sources.creator: Dane Poset
image_primary_sources.description: The W16 engine installed on the Veyron chassis — showing the packaging challenge of fitting four turbochargers and ten radiators around the powerplant.
image_primary_sources.chicago_caption: Dane Poset, *W16 Engine on a Bugatti Veyron Chassis*, 2010, Wikimedia Commons, CC BY-SA 3.0, https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:W-16_on_a_Bugatti_Veyron_-_panoramio.jpg.
image_primary_sources.chicago_bibliography: Poset, Dane. *W16 Engine on a Bugatti Veyron Chassis*. Photograph. 2010. Wikimedia Commons. CC BY-SA 3.0. https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:W-16_on_a_Bugatti_Veyron_-_panoramio.jpg.
image_primary_sources.chicago_footnote_short: Poset, *W16 Engine on a Bugatti Veyron Chassis*.

notes_on_use: Period artifacts (magazine scans, delivery photos, press kits) are used under fair-use educational exemption for this UTK museum exhibit.
Specimen photographs are Wikimedia Commons CC / PD images — attribution per the license strings.
All chicago_caption / chicago_bibliography strings are pre-formatted.
When rendering captions, link to source_page (not source_url) so the viewer lands on the article that provides provenance.
